title: feat(loki): new domain for read-only Loki LogQL queries
description:
Add a new `loki` domain mirroring `prom`. Loki is the natural log-side counterpart to Prometheus on the metric side, and the incident-triage path (alert → metric → log → node) currently bottoms out at `k8s logs` for live pods only — there's no way to ask Loki about retention-window logs.

## Shape

Mirror `prom` exactly:
- `ureq` + rustls, sync, no tokio runtime added
- `LOKI_URL` env var; `--url` per-command override
- Chokepoint at `src/loki/client.rs` (ureq::Agent + GET-only enforcement via grep test, same forbidden tokens as prom: `.post(`/`.put(`/`.patch(`/`.delete(`)
- Cargo feature `loki`, on by default (measure binary size impact first; if non-trivial, opt-in)

## Commands

### `sak loki query <logql>`
Instant query against `/loki/api/v1/query`. Output: per-stream JSON lines or `--format tsv` (`timestamp<TAB>labels<TAB>line`).

### `sak loki query-range <logql>`
Range query against `/loki/api/v1/query_range`. Reuse `prom::duration::parse_duration` for `--since`/`--start`/`--end`/`--step`.

### `sak loki labels`
GET `/loki/api/v1/labels` — list all label names.

### `sak loki label-values <name>`
GET `/loki/api/v1/label/<name>/values`.

### `sak loki series`
GET `/loki/api/v1/series?match[]=<selector>&start=...&end=...` — list series matching a selector.

## Implementation notes

- Loki's response envelope is `{status, data: {resultType, result}}` for instant/range and `{status, data: [...]}` for labels/series — similar but not identical to prom's. Add a `LokiClient::get_loki` helper to unwrap; fall back to `get_json` for raw.
- 404 → `Ok(None)` mirroring k8s/lxc/docker.
- Read-only enforcement: grep test forbids mutation methods outside `client.rs`. Loki has admin write endpoints (`/loki/api/v1/delete`) so this is a real guardrail.
- Reuse `src/prom/duration.rs` via a `pub use` re-export, or promote to `src/duration.rs` for cross-domain use.

## Out of scope (file separately)

- Tail mode (`/loki/api/v1/tail`) is a websocket — different transport, defer.
- Kubernetes service auto-discovery + transparent port-forward — same deferral as for prom.

title: feat(git): ahead-behind, churn, age commands
description:
Three small additions to round out the git domain. Bundled because they share a revwalk-with-diff helper.

## Commands

### `sak git ahead-behind <ref1> [ref2]`

How many commits each ref has that the other doesn't. `ref2` defaults to `HEAD`. Output:

```
ahead<TAB><N>
behind<TAB><N>
```

One call: `Repository::graph_ahead_behind(oid1, oid2)`. ~10 lines.

### `sak git churn [path...]`

Commit count per file across history. Output: `count<TAB>path`, sorted by count descending. Filter to a subtree by passing paths. Flag `--since <date>` to bound the walk.

Useful for hotspot detection during code review ("what file changes most?").

Implementation: walk `repo.revwalk()`, then for each commit walk the diff against its first parent, increment a `HashMap<PathBuf, u64>`. Skip merge commits unless `--include-merges`.

### `sak git age [path...]`

Last-touched timestamp per file. Output: `timestamp<TAB>days_ago<TAB>path`, sorted by timestamp ascending (oldest first). Useful for dead-code triage.

Implementation: revwalk from HEAD, for each commit record file deltas, first-seen-wins semantics. Stop walking once every tracked file has a timestamp (track tracked-file set via `repo.index()`).

## Common flags

- `--limit` — output truncation via `BoundedWriter`
- `--since <date>` — bound the walk (churn, age)
- `--include-merges` — include merge commits in deltas (default: skip)

## Notes

- All three use `git2` (no shelling out).
- Read-only by construction.
- `ahead-behind` is trivial; `churn` and `age` share a revwalk-with-diff helper that probably belongs in `src/git/walk.rs`.
- No README hook update needed — these aren't existing git read verbs that the hook regex matches.

title: feat(k8s): logs command
description:
## Context

The most-used read command on the entire Kubernetes API. The single biggest gap in the current `sak k8s` surface. Anything an LLM does to debug a cluster eventually ends up here.

## Command

### `sak k8s logs <pod>`

Args:
- `<pod>` — pod name
- `-c, --container <name>` — pick a container; required when the pod has multiple unless `--all-containers` is set
- `--all-containers` — emit logs from every container in the pod, prefixed with `[container]`
- `--tail <n>` — last N lines (maps to `LogParams::tail_lines`)
- `--since <duration>` — e.g. `10m`, `1h`, `2h30m` (maps to `LogParams::since_seconds`); parse with a small humantime-style helper, no new dep
- `-p, --previous` — read the previous container instance (`LogParams::previous = true`)
- `--grep <pattern>` — line-oriented regex filter applied client-side. Cheap, and saves the LLM from piping through `sak fs grep` for every query
- `-n, --namespace <ns>`
- `--limit <n>` — line cap via `BoundedWriter`

Output: log lines, one per line, optionally `[container] ` prefixed when `--all-containers`. Through `BoundedWriter`.

Exit codes: 0 found, 1 if no lines (pod exists but no logs / no matches after `--grep`), 2 on error.

## Implementation notes

- `Api::<Pod>::logs(name, &LogParams)` is the kube call. It is **not** a mutation method, but the chokepoint grep test currently forbids `kube::Api` outside `client.rs`, so this needs a new helper:

  ```rust
  pub async fn pod_logs(
      client: &Client,
      namespace: &str,
      pod: &str,
      lp: &LogParams,
  ) -> Result<String> { ... }
  ```

  Add it to `client.rs` next to `list_dyn` / `get_dyn`. Pod-typed `Api<Pod>` is OK *inside* `client.rs` — the grep test only forbids `kube::Api` mentions in *other* files.
- For `--all-containers`, fetch the Pod via the existing `get_dyn` first, walk `spec.containers[*].name` (and optionally `initContainers` later), and call `pod_logs` once per container, prefixing each line.
- Streaming vs buffered: kube exposes `log_stream` for true streaming. For sak, buffered (`logs()` returning a String) is fine — the `--limit` and `--tail` flags bound the output, and the rest of sak is synchronous-batch by design. If a real need for streaming surfaces later, file a follow-up.
- `--grep` uses `regex::Regex` (already a sak dep). Compile once, apply per line.

## Files

**Create:**
- `src/k8s/logs.rs`

**Modify:**
- `src/k8s/client.rs` — add `pod_logs` chokepoint helper. Use `kube::api::LogParams`.
- `src/k8s/mod.rs` — add `Logs` variant and dispatch.

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo test --features k8s` passes (unit-test the `--since` duration parser on at least `10s`, `5m`, `2h30m`, plus an invalid input).
- Chokepoint grep test still passes — `pod_logs` lives only in `client.rs`.
- `cargo clippy --all-features --all-targets` clean.
- Manual:
  - `cargo run -- k8s logs <pod> -n <ns>`
  - `cargo run -- k8s logs <pod> --tail 20 --grep ERROR`
  - `cargo run -- k8s logs <multi-container-pod> --all-containers`
- Patch-level version bump.
close_reason: Implemented sak k8s logs. New chokepoint helper client::pod_logs (Api<Pod>::logs) keeps the kube::Api mention confined to client.rs so the existing grep test still passes. Command supports -c/--container, --all-containers (auto-prefixes lines with [container]), --tail, --since with a small humantime parser (s/m/h/d, compound forms like 2h30m), -p/--previous, --grep regex filter via the existing regex dep, -n/--namespace, --limit. Auto-picks the only container when --container is omitted on a single-container pod, errors helpfully when there are multiple. Unit tests cover parse_since for valid/compound/invalid inputs and container_names extraction. Patch version bump to 0.8.3. README hook regex extended to redirect 'kubectl logs' and the kubectl pass-through note updated. README features list and main.rs --help quick-start examples include the new command. Default and --no-default-features builds, tests, clippy, and fmt all pass.

title: feat(gh): client chokepoint + dispatch foundation
description:
Foundation for the gh domain. Blocks every other gh command issue.

## Deliverables

- `src/gh/mod.rs` — Clap subcommand dispatch, empty initially.
- `src/gh/client.rs` — sole chokepoint for `Command::new("gh")`:
  - `READ_ONLY_VERBS: &[(&str, Option<&str>)]` allowlist (see epic body).
  - `invoke(noun, verb, args, ...)` returns `Output { stdout, stderr, status }`, mirroring `src/talos/client.rs::Output` and `src/nix/client.rs`.
  - `invoke_ok(...)` returns `Ok(stdout)` on success, `Err` on nonzero exit with trimmed stderr surfaced.
  - **`gh api` method guard**: when the noun is `api`, scan `args` for `-X` / `--method` and reject any value other than `GET` (case-insensitive). Bare `gh api <endpoint>` (no method flag) defaults to GET and is allowed. `-X GET` is accepted (redundant but correct).
  - Inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` with:
    - `no_gh_invocations_outside_client_module` — copy of `src/talos/client.rs::no_talosctl_invocations_outside_client_module` with forbidden tokens `"gh"` (as a quoted literal) and `Command::new(`, exempting comment lines. Watch out for false positives — `gh` is a short string. Use `"gh"` with the quote marks (not bare `gh`) to scope the match to actual literals.
    - `rejects_mutating_verb` — assert that `invoke("pr", Some("merge"), ...)`, `invoke("issue", Some("close"), ...)`, `invoke("repo", Some("create"), ...)`, `invoke("workflow", Some("run"), ...)`, `invoke("auth", Some("login"), ...)`, `invoke("secret", Some("set"), ...)` all return errors mentioning the allowlist.
    - `rejects_non_get_api_method` — assert `invoke("api", None, &["repos/x/y", "-X", "POST"])` and `&["repos/x/y", "--method", "delete"]` are both rejected.
    - `accepts_explicit_get` — `invoke("api", None, &["repos/x/y", "-X", "GET"])` is allowed.
- `src/main.rs` — add `gh` to the domain dispatch.
- `Cargo.toml` — bump version per CLAUDE.md (minor: new domain).
- README.md — add `gh` to the domain list.

## Auth note

`gh` reads `GH_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` from the environment, or `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml` populated by `gh auth login`. No sak-side plumbing needed; the chokepoint passes env through unchanged.

## Out of scope

- Any individual command — they're their own child issues.
- Hook integration (`src/hook/claude_code.rs::check_gh`) — folded into the first command issue that introduces shadowing of a `gh` verb.
close_reason: Foundation landed: src/gh/client.rs (READ_ONLY_VERBS allowlist of (noun, Option<verb>) pairs, Output struct, invoke / invoke_ok, gh-api method guard rejecting non-GET -X/--method values, chokepoint grep test, plus rejects_mutating_verb / rejects_non_get_api_method / accepts_explicit_get / rejects_dangling_method_flag / rejects_unknown_verb_under_allowed_noun / allows_verbless_noun tests). src/gh/mod.rs defines GhCommand (empty until first child) and run(). Wired into src/main.rs (mod gh, Command::Gh, dispatch). Version bumped to 0.14.0. README + CLAUDE.md domain lists updated. cargo fmt, cargo clippy (default + --no-default-features), cargo test (default + --no-default-features) all clean. Child issues sak-llm-gt1 (api) and the rest are now unblocked. Note: client.rs carries a module-level #![allow(dead_code)] until the first child consumes invoke; remove it then.

title: feat(nix): references command
description:
Wraps `nix-store --query` with one of `--references` (default, direct refs out), `--referrers` (direct refs in), or `--requisites` (transitive closure). Output: one store path per line.

Flags:
- `--direct` (default), `--referrers`, `--closure` — mutually exclusive
- `--limit`

Note: `nix-store` is a separate binary from `nix` — the chokepoint needs to either grow a second-binary path or this command can be re-shaped to use `nix store info <path>` + walking its JSON output. Prefer the latter to keep the chokepoint single-binary; if `nix store` doesn't expose the same data in modern nix, add a second chokepoint binary (`nix-store`) with its own allowlist.

Hook: redirect `nix-store --query --references|--referrers|--requisites` → sak.

title: feat(gh): issue-view command
description:
Wraps `gh issue view <number> --json <fields>`. Inspect a single issue's metadata, body, comments.

## Command

### `sak gh issue-view <number>`

Flags:
- `--repo <owner/name>`
- `--fields <a,b,c>` — default `number,title,author,body,state,labels,assignees,milestone,createdAt,updatedAt,comments`
- `--format <tsv|json>` (default `json` — same rationale as `pr-view`)
- `--limit`

Output: JSON passthrough by default; TSV emits scalar fields as `key<TAB>value`.

Hook: redirect `gh issue view` → sak.
close_reason: Landed sak gh issue-view. src/gh/issue_view.rs: required positional <issue> (number/URL; precedes --json), --repo, --fields (default number,title,author,body,state,labels,assignees,milestone,createdAt,updatedAt,comments — all validated against 'gh issue view --json' field list; forwarded verbatim), --format tsv|json (default JSON since issues have nested arrays labels/assignees/comments), --limit. Runs via chokepoint invoke_ok('issue', Some('view'), ...) and reuses emit_single from render.rs (no new rendering code). build_argv split out; 4 tests. Hook: extended check_gh to redirect 'gh issue view' -> 'sak gh issue-view'; 'gh issue develop'/'close' and other unshadowed verbs pass. Tests gh_issue_view_blocks + updated gh_mutations_and_unshadowed_allow (moved 'gh issue view' from allow to block). Docs: main.rs QUICK_START example, README + CLAUDE.md dogfood bullets. Version 0.14.8 -> 0.14.9 (patch). cargo fmt / clippy (default + --no-default-features) / test (default + --no-default-features) all clean. Live-smoke-tested against cli/cli issue 13464: tsv field<TAB>value clean (empty assignees/milestone render as '-', labels comma-joined), json pipes into sak json query, hook block/allow correct.
